```{r}
subset_salmonella_host <- subset_taxa(ps_cheesecloth_incubation, Phylum %in% c("Pseudomonadota"))
as.data.frame(tax_table(subset_salmonella_host))

```

#DNA Extraction kit comparisons

```{r}
#Filtering samples by condition - Host-DNA depletion
samples.out

dnakits_originalnames <- c("PRMgDNAUn", "PrMigDNA1", "PrMigDNA2", "PrMigDNA3", "QDnBT1", "QDnBT2", "QDnBT3", "QDnBTSp", "QDnBTUn", "ZymoMiPr1", "ZymoMiPr2", "ZymoMiPr3", "ZymoMiPrSp", "ZymoMIPrUn")

dnakits_originalnames

DNAkits <- dnakits_originalnames[dnakits_originalnames %in% samples.out]

DNAkits

DNAkits <- gsub("^PrMigDNA", "Presto Mini gDNA ", DNAkits)
DNAkits <- gsub("^PRMgDNA", "Presto Mini gDNA ", DNAkits)

DNAkits <- gsub("^QDnBT", "Qiagen DNeasy Blood & Tissue ", DNAkits)
DNAkits <- gsub("^ZymoMiPr", "ZymoResearch DNA Miniprep ", DNAkits)
DNAkits <- gsub("^ZymoMIPr", "ZymoResearch DNA Miniprep ", DNAkits)

DNAkits <- gsub("Sp", "Spiked", DNAkits)
DNAkits <- gsub("Un", "Unspiked", DNAkits)

DNAkits

```

#Create data table for Host-DNA depletion

```{r}
DNAkit_type <- str_extract(DNAkits, "^(ZymoResearch DNA Miniprep|Qiagen DNeasy Blood & Tissue|Presto Mini gDNA)")

DNAkit_type

Spiking <- str_extract(DNAkits, "Unspiked|Spiked")
Spiking
Spiking <- ifelse(is.na(Spiking), "Chicken Rinse", Spiking)

Number <- str_extract(DNAkits, "1|2|3")
Number
Number <- ifelse(is.na(Number), "Control", Number)

samdf3 <- data.frame("DNA Kit"=DNAkit_type, "Sample"=Spiking, "Number/Type"=Number)

samdf3

rownames(samdf3) <- (dnakits_originalnames)


DNAkits2 <- phyloseq(otu_table(seqtab.nochim, taxa_are_rows=FALSE), sample_data(samdf3), tax_table(taxa))

DNAkits2

sample_data(DNAkits2)$Name <- paste(
  sample_data(DNAkits2)$Number,
  sample_data(DNAkits2)$Sample, 
  sep = " - "
)

```

#Host-DNA depletion testing - filtered and filtered centrifuge samples

```{r}
DNAkits2 <- tax_glom(DNAkits2, taxrank = "Family")

# Select top 20 most abundant taxa
top20_dnakits <- names(sort(taxa_sums(DNAkits2), decreasing=TRUE))[1:20]

# Normalize abundances
DNAkits2.top20 <- transform_sample_counts(DNAkits2, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
DNAkits2.top20

# Keep only top 20 taxa
DNAkits2.top20 <- prune_taxa(top20_dnakits, DNAkits2.top20)

# Aggregate at Family level to combine taxa from the same Family
DNAkits2.top20_family <- tax_glom(DNAkits2.top20, taxrank = "Family")

DNAkits2.top20_family

colours <- c("#72e5ef", "#c23165", "#aef815", "#6f3a93", "#56a221", "#cf60f3", "#2cf52b", "#fe16f4", "#93f993", "#bf7d9a", "#1c5f1e", "#f2c8e8", "#145a6a", "#d5ec9e", "#7c08c5", "#f3d426", "#0494fb", "#96a467", "#8e80fb", "#0ba47e")

colours

# Generate the bar plot
p_DNA <- plot_bar(DNAkits2.top20_family, 
              x = "Name", fill = "Family") + 
  facet_wrap(~DNA.Kit, scales = "free_x") +
     theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 0.5))

p_DNA

jpeg(file="taxa20_DNAkits.jpg", height=1000, width=1800)
# Final plot with clearer color grouping
p_DNA +  # outline between segments
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +  
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black") + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),  # for facet labels
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=colours)
dev.off()

```

```{r}

ps_DNAkits_ra <- transform_sample_counts(DNAkits2.top20_family, function(x) 100 * x / sum(x))

p_DNAkits_ra <- plot_bar(ps_DNAkits_ra, 
              x = "Name", fill = "Family") + 
  facet_wrap(~DNA.Kit, scales = "free_x") +
     theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 0.5))

p_DNAkits_ra


#Relative abundance plot
jpeg(file="taxa20_DNAkits_ra.jpg", height=1000, width=1800)
p_DNAkits_ra +  # outline between segments
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +  # Increase base text size for everything
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),  # for facet labels
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
dev.off()

```

```{r}
head(track)

track_data <- data.frame(track)
track_data


library(pheatmap)


track_data


pheatmap(track_data,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "blue"))(100),
         main = "Read Counts at Each Step",
         fontsize_row = 10,     # adjust text size for row labels
         fontsize_col = 10)     # adjust text size for column labels



# Convert to a matrix (1 row or 1 column)
nonchim_matrix <- as.matrix(track_data$nonchim)

# Optionally set row/column names
rownames(nonchim_matrix) <- rownames(track_data)
colnames(nonchim_matrix) <- "nonchim"

# Check rownames and colnames
rownames(nonchim_matrix)
colnames(nonchim_matrix)

# If your sample names are the rownames of track_data
nonchim_matrix <- as.matrix(track_data$nonchim)

# Add rownames if missing
rownames(nonchim_matrix) <- rownames(track_data)

# Or, if you have sample names in another column (say, "SampleID")
rownames(nonchim_matrix) <- track_data$SampleID



# Then plot!
pheatmap(t(nonchim_matrix), 
         cluster_rows = FALSE, 
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         color = colorRampPalette(c("white", "#23238c"))(100),
         labels_row = colnames(nonchim_matrix),
         labels_col = rownames(nonchim_matrix))



```

```{r}

library(aplot)

combined_plot <- pheatmap %>% 
  insert_right(p, width = 0.25)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(patchwork)  # for combining ggplots

# Assuming nonchim_matrix is already defined
# Let's transpose it, since pheatmap(t(nonchim_matrix)) was used
heatmap_data <- t(nonchim_matrix)

# Convert matrix to long format for ggplot2
df_heatmap <- melt(heatmap_data)

# Rename for clarity
colnames(df_heatmap) <- c("Row", "Col", "Value")

# Create the heatmap with ggplot2
p_heatmap <- ggplot(df_heatmap, aes(x = Col, y = Row, fill = Value)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("white", "#23238c")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    axis.title = element_blank()
  )

p_heatmap


```

```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Convert phyloseq object to data.frame
df <- phyloseq::psmelt(ps_enrichment_testing.top20_enrichment_testing)

# Inspect the structure
head(df)

gg_p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = Incubation, y = Abundance, fill = Family)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", linewidth=0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~Broth, scales = "free_x") +  # Facet by Broth
  theme_bw(base_size = 16) +  # Base font size
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 14, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 16),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Incubation",
    y = "Abundance",
    fill = "Family"
  )
gg_p$data$Incubation <- factor(gg_p$data$Incubation, levels = sort(as.numeric(unique(gg_p$data$Incubation))))

gg_p


```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

# Extract OTU table (samples as columns)
otu_mat <- as(otu_table(ps_enrichment_testing.top20_enrichment_testing), "matrix")

# Make sure taxa are rows
if(!taxa_are_rows(ps_enrichment_testing.top20_enrichment_testing)) {
  otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
}

# Inspect it
dim(otu_mat)
head(otu_mat)

```

```{r}
# Extract sample metadata from the phyloseq object
sample_metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps_enrichment_testing.top20_enrichment_testing))
head(sample_metadata)

# Add the Sample column to sample_metadata to make joining easier
sample_metadata$Sample <- rownames(sample_metadata)

# Join df_long with sample metadata on the Sample column
df_long <- df_long %>%
  left_join(sample_metadata, by = "Sample")

head(df_long)

# Summarize total reads by Sample, ignoring Family
df_total_reads <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Broth, Incubation) %>%
  summarise(Total_Reads = sum(Reads, na.rm = TRUE))

# View the summarized data
head(df_total_reads)


df_long$Incubation <- factor(df_long$Incubation, levels = sort(unique(df_long$Incubation)))

```

```{r}

df_long$Reads_log <- log10(df_long$Reads + 1)

# Extract the prefix (letters) and numeric parts
df_long$Prefix <- gsub("[0-9]", "", df_long$Sample)
df_long$Sample_num <- as.numeric(gsub("[A-Za-z]+", "", df_long$Sample))

# Arrange the data by Prefix and then Sample_num
df_long <- df_long %>%
  arrange(Prefix, Sample_num)

# Now reorder the Sample factor based on the sorted order
df_long$Sample <- factor(df_long$Sample, levels = unique(df_long$Sample))


# Now plot the heatmap with ordered sample names
p_heatmap <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = Sample, y = Family, fill = Reads)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +  # White gridlines between tiles
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#23238c") +  # Customize colors
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  ) +
  labs(x = "Sample", y = "Family", fill = "Read Count")

# Show the heatmap
p_heatmap

```

```{r}

combined_plot <- p_heatmap + gg_p + plot_layout(ncol = 2)

combined_plot

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Summarize total reads per sample
sample_reads <- df_long %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarize(Total_Reads = sum(Reads)) %>%
  arrange(Total_Reads)

# View the summarized table
sample_reads

sample_reads$Sample <- factor(sample_reads$Sample, levels = sample_reads$Sample)

library(ggplot2)

p_total_reads_heatmap <- ggplot(sample_reads, aes(x = Sample, y = 1, fill = Total_Reads)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#23238c") +  # Adjust colors if needed
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),  # No y-axis because it's just one row
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  ) +
  labs(x = "Sample", fill = "Total Reads", title = "Total Read Count per Sample")

p_total_reads_heatmap

```

```{r}
library(patchwork)

# Assume you already have:
# p_main = your main ggplot (e.g., bar plot, heatmap of families)
# p_total_reads_heatmap = the heatmap or barplot of total reads per sample

# Combine them vertically
combined_plot_totalread <- gg_p / p_total_reads_heatmap  # '/' stacks them vertically

# Show the plot
combined_plot_totalread




```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Example: Clean top plot (remove x-axis)
p_main_clean <- gg_p +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()
  )

# Example: Clean heatmap (remove y axis, optional)
p_heatmap_clean <- p_total_reads_heatmap +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank()
  )

# Combine with patchwork, sharing the x-axis
combined_plot <- p_main_clean / p_heatmap_clean +
  plot_layout(heights = c(4, 1))  # Control how much space each plot gets

combined_plot

library(cowplot)

aligned_plots <- align_plots(p_main_clean, p_heatmap_clean, align = 'v', axis = 'lr')

combined_plot <- plot_grid(
  aligned_plots[[1]],
  aligned_plots[[2]],
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(4, 1)
)

combined_plot

```
